question_title: What will be the value of "30-Year Fixed Rate FHA Mortgage Index" on 2026-04-21?
summary: The 30-Year Fixed Rate FHA Mortgage Index (OBMMIFHA30YF) was 6.067 on April 9, 2026, the last observed data point. The series has been in a stable regime since March 30 with a mean of ~6.095. News from April 12 indicates mortgage rates are declining (30-year fixed down 7bps from prior weekend), the 10-year Treasury yield is stable at 4.29%, and a Bankrate poll shows 56% of experts expect rates to fall next week. A Monte Carlo simulation using empirical daily volatility (0.052) over 8 business days, with a weighted mixture of four drift scenarios (regime drift, zero drift, consensus decline, macro risk), produces a median around 6.05 with 80% CI of 5.93-6.18. The distribution reflects genuine uncertainty from daily rate volatility over the 8-day horizon, balanced between the slight downward consensus and upside macro risk from tariff/inflation uncertainty.

factors.description: Last observed value is 6.067 on Apr 9; current regime mean is 6.095 since Mar 30. Strong anchor for the forecast center.
Slight downward drift in regime (-0.006/day over 8 obs), plus news reports rates declining week of Apr 7-12. Bankrate poll: 56% expect rates to fall next week.
10-year Treasury at 4.29% (Apr 9), stable to slightly declining, supports steady-to-lower mortgage rates
Tariff/trade war uncertainty and inflation concerns could push rates up - macro tail risk factor
Historical daily volatility of ~0.052 over 8 business days produces natural uncertainty width; sqrt(8)*0.052 ≈ 0.147 std dev

reasoning:
**Forecast Summary: OBMMIFHA30YF on 2026-04-21**

| Percentile | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| P10 | 5.86% |
| P25 | 5.95% |
| P50 | 6.05% |
| P75 | 6.15% |
| P90 | 6.24% |

**Key reasoning:**
- **Anchor**: Last observed value was 6.067% on April 9, within a stable regime averaging 6.095% since March 30
- **Trend**: Slight downward drift supported by news (rates falling week of Apr 7-12), declining Treasury yields, and expert consensus (56% expect further declines)
- **Uncertainty**: 8 business days of daily volatility (~0.052/day) drives a natural 80% CI width of ~0.32 percentage points
- **Risk**: Tariff/inflation macro shocks could push rates higher, but the base case favors stability to slight decline
- **Method**: Monte Carlo mixture model with four weighted drift scenarios and empirical volatility
sources_consulted: [Mortgage and refinance interest rates today, April 12, 2026: Rates fall further since last weekend](https://finance.yahoo.com/personal-finance/mortgages/article/mortgage-refinance-interest-rates-today-sunday-april-12-2026-100000017.html)
resolution_criteria: Resolves to the value found on the FRED API for the series OBMMIFHA30YF once the data is published.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will paginate through the data found at the API endpoint `https://api.stlouisfed.org/fred/series/observations?series_id=OBMMIFHA30YF`. This endpoint includes the latest data for the series. The latest revised data will be used when the resolution is assessed. The datapoint matching 2026-04-21 will be used to determine the resolution of this question.

A datapoint matches if:
1. The series is updated daily and the date of the datapoint is within 1 day previous to the resolution date.
2. The series is updated weekly and the date of the datapoint is within 7 days previous to the resolution date.
3. The series is updated monthly and the date of the datapoint is within 31 days previous to the resolution date.

If the datapoint is clearly skipped, or no datapoint is found after 2 weeks of checking then the question will be annulled.
